# simulate_admixture { #tspaint.simulate_admixture }

```python
tspaint.simulate_admixture(
    demography,
    *,
    n_query=10,
    n_reference=10,
    samples=None,
    sequence_length=1000000.0,
    recombination_rate=1e-08,
    ploidy=2,
    mutation_rate=None,
    random_seed=42,
)
```

Simulate an admixture from a **role-tagged demography**, returning a :class:`Simulation`.

Pass a demography from :func:`admixture_demography` (or any ``admixture_demography_*`` variant):
its populations carry role metadata (query / source / reference + ancestry state), so this
samples the query and reference panels, runs the coalescent, optionally lays down mutations, and
packages the queries, reference ``labels`` and the census ``truth`` — the caller never touches a
population-name constant.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**demography**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[msprime](`msprime`).[Demography](`msprime.Demography`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A role-tagged demography (see :func:`admixture_demography`).

<code>[**n_query**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of query (admixed) individuals to sample.

<code>[**n_reference**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of individuals sampled from **each** reference / source population.

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Explicit ``{population_name: n_individuals}`` sampling, overriding ``n_query`` / ``n_reference`` — used by the variant wrappers to sample e.g. unequal pure / impure panels.

<code>[**sequence_length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**recombination_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**random_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**mutation_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If given, lay down mutations at this per-base rate (:func:`tspaint.io.add_mutations`); the returned ``ts`` then carries sites. ``None`` (default) leaves the bare ancestry.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Simulation](`tspaint.sim.Simulation`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``.ts`` (mutated iff ``mutation_rate`` given), ``.queries``, ``.labels``, ``.truth_states`` and ``.sample_sets``.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> sim = simulate_admixture(admixture_demography(), n_query=5, n_reference=5,
...                          sequence_length=1e5, random_seed=1)
>>> sim.ts.num_samples
30
```
